title: Reader Outcomes, Repository Baseline, Static Architecture, and Evidence · Program Brief: Repository Baseline
summary: A team says “build a blog generator” but has not defined readers, publishing authority, content ownership, supported routes, accessibility needs, repository state, or acceptance evidence. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the repository baseline increment in reader, repository, content, architecture, and evidence charter. Learners progress throug…
objectives: Inspect status, revision, configuration origin, ignores, content licenses, generated files, and existing gates before editing.
Distinguish build-time transformation, immutable output, browser execution, origin hosting, CDN behavior, and external services.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Predict Repository Baseline · Theory Repository Baseline
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory publishing team handles case 8498 during a large technical reference incremental rebuild. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the publication charter on reader value, repository state, accessibility, and content authority; reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Inspect status, revision, configuration origin, ignores, content licenses, generated files, and existing gates before editing.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example publishing team handles case 8498 during a large technical reference incremental rebuild. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the publication charter on reader value, repository state, accessibility, and content authority; reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Inspect status, revision, configuration origin, ignores, content licenses, generated files, and existing gates before editing. A defensible solution applies Repository Baseline, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to reader, repository, content, architecture, and evidence charter.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-outcomes-repo-architecture-4
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-outcomes-repo-architecture-4.
# Case 1655: an accessibility-led community newsroom release.
# Operating constraint: reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval.
# Changed case: delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests.
def worked_static_site_outcomes_repo_architecture_4(reader_task: str = "find-current-guidance", acceptance_cases: int = 8, repository_clean: bool = True, accessibility_plan: bool = True, content_authorized: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "readerworked4",
        "source1655",
        "parse4dekrow",
        "routeworked41655",
        "artifact1655worked4",
        "recovery4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if not reader_task.strip() or acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-outcome"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not accessibility_plan:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "accessibility-gap"
    if not content_authorized:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "content-authority"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-site-charter"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python static-site decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected source, parse, route, render, asset, accessibility, cache, deployment, or recovery case, then state which filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-repository-baseline-1s0f2j
# Competency: ssg-repository-baseline.
# Case 8755: a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment.
# Operating constraint: retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity.
# Changed case: delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests.
def evidence_ssg_repository_baseline_1s0f2j(reader_task: str = "find-current-guidance", acceptance_cases: int = 8, repository_clean: bool = True, accessibility_plan: bool = True, content_authorized: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1s0f2j = "1s0f2j-j2f0s1-8755"
    assert evidence_variant_1s0f2j
    evidence_axes_1s0f2j = (
        "reader1s0f2j",
        "source8755",
        "parsej2f0s1",
        "route1s0f2j8755",
        "artifact87551s0f2j",
        "recoveryj2f0s18755",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1s0f2j)) == 6
    if not reader_task.strip() or acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-outcome"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not accessibility_plan:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "accessibility-gap"
    if not content_authorized:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "content-authority"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-site-charter"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Repository Baseline model · Theory Repository Baseline
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Repository Baseline: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 3066 during an offline-first field documentation build. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the publication charter on reader value, repository state, accessibility, and content authority; reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval; then replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Inspect status, revision, configuration origin, ignores, content licenses, generated files, and existing gates before editing.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Repository Baseline model · Theory Repository Baseline.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Repository Baseline model · Theory Repository Baseline, keep Repository Baseline connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Inspect status, revision, configuration origin, ignores, content licenses, generated files, and existing gates before editing.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Repository Baseline model · Theory Repository Baseline, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Repository Baseline precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Repository Baseline model · Theory Repository Baseline, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 3066 during an offline-first field documentation build. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the publication charter on reader value, repository state, accessibility, and content authority; reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval; then replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Inspect status, revision, configuration origin, ignores, content licenses, generated files, and existing gates before editing.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Repository Baseline model · Theory Repository Baseline, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: Generated output can be committed safely without first identifying its source and owner.
